brand.name: Your Brand Name
brand.one_line: One sentence describing who you help and how.

voice._comment: voice-fingerprint.md = output of the `voiceprint` skill (github.com/brie-wieselman/voiceprint). content-rules.md = your brand/content canon; the writer reads sections from it BY HEADING (see agents/brand-context.js).

canva_templates._comment: Map each content type to YOUR OWN saved Canva brand-template IDs. Get these from your Canva account — never reuse anyone else's.

topics._comment: manual = you supply the niche + topic ideas below (no paid APIs needed). scraper = the engine mines trending topics via your own paid API keys (see scraper block). Start with manual.
manual_topics: A specific topic or angle you want content about

scraper._comment: OPTIONAL. Requires your own paid RapidAPI + YouTube Data API keys in .env. Leave disabled to run fully on manual topics.

platforms._comment: Turn on only the platforms you actually post to. Each needs a connected account in your scheduler (see scheduler block).

articles._comment: Finished articles are written here as markdown + HTML for you to publish anywhere (blog, Substack, etc.). No website integration required.

approval._comment: approve = nothing schedules until you reply an approval (safest, default). auto = graded content schedules automatically. You can set this per run.

schedule._comment: When the pipeline runs. Cron-like. This does NOT publish — it produces + queues; publishing follows your approval.mode.

scheduler._comment: Blotato is the supported multi-platform scheduler (one API posts to IG/TikTok/LinkedIn/Threads/etc.). Put your Blotato account IDs per platform here; key goes in .env.

data._comment: The engine uses a Google Sheet as its database (calendar, research, performance log). Copy the template sheet (see ONBOARDING.md) and put its ID here.
